svg.title: Spreadsheet-Native Returns

g.text: The Returns sheet keeps creation and queue review together, while order-return state remains a visibly adjacent follow-up rather than an implicit side effect.

svg.text: The queue shows created returns and their downstream accounting or inventory evidence without making the inspector the primary workflow.
Return creation stays explicit and reviewable. Restock is a visible decision, not a silent consequence.
The compose panel stays adjacent to the queue so operators can choose line items, reason, notes, and restock intent without leaving the sheet.
The compose surface covers GF-009. It should not silently trigger GF-012 order status changes without explicit confirmation.
This band keeps the staged return lifecycle and the two return truths visible so the artboard does not collapse them into one ambiguous workflow.
Owns return record creation, approve or reject review, receive, process, optional restock, refund follow-up, and GL reversal visibility.
Owns shipped-or-delivered order state transitions and workbook-side actions like mark-as-returned, restock, vendor return, and vendor-return options.

g.text: Selected RET-404 · Enter process · Cmd/Ctrl+O open order
GF-009 is the primary Returns sheet workflow. Refunds and workbook return actions stay visible as linked context.
